sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.87 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Let${ M}$be a variety of groups and let$G_r$be a free$r$-generator group in$M$. A subset$S G_r$is called a test set\/ if every endomorphism of$G_r$identical on$S$is an automorphism. The minimum of the cardinalities of test sets is called the test rank of$G_r$. Suppose that the test rank of$G_r$is$r$for every$r 1$. a) Is it true that${ M}$is an abelian variety? b) Suppose that${ M}$is not a periodi...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
